criteria.description: Every source entry is referenced in the rendered content (by entryId or stable title+cid reference).

Entries whose type REQUIRES signing carry a valid content_signature. Required: (a) entryType in ['identity','soul'] (always), (b) entryType='procedural' with a 'risk:high' tag. Optional (do NOT penalize when unsigned): entryType in ['episodic','reflection'], 'semantic' (signing recommended only for architecture/security decisions), 'procedural' with risk:low or risk:medium or missing risk tag. Score 1 if all required-signed entries are signed; score fractionally by (required_signed_ok / required_signed_total) when some required entries lack signatures. If there are zero required-signed entries in the source pack, score 1.

No factual claims in the rendered content that cannot be traced to a source entry. Enumerate per-claim assertions: for each distinct factual claim in the rendered content, return {id, text, passed, evidence}. PASS requires citing a source entry id and quoting the supporting span; FAIL requires quoting the offending claim verbatim. The criterion score is 1 iff every assertion passes.

rubric.preamble: Evaluate whether the rendered markdown faithfully represents the source pack's entries, with complete coverage and no external claims. Grounding uses per-claim binary assertions with evidence — see #999.
